bio: Chilean artist, researcher, and entrepreneur. Graduate of NYU's Interactive Telecommunications Program (ITP). Previously a researcher at NYU. Named to TIME100 Most Influential People in AI (2023) and Forbes 30 Under 30 - Art & Style (2020). Background in art and technology.

Greek AI researcher, software engineer, and artist. Graduate of NYU's ITP program. Previously worked at Quantcast, Chartbeat, Zocdoc, and IBM Research as Machine Learning Researcher in Computer Vision. Expert in video generation models, multimodal AI, and deep learning.

Chilean designer and creative technologist. Graduate of NYU's ITP program. Background in design, film, and advertising. Previously worked in creative technology and design. Led Runway's design vision and user experience.

Danish entrepreneur. BSc in Computer Science and Business Informatics from IT-Universitetet i København, studied at Stanford University (2015). Previously founded RBLI (digital strategy consultancy), worked at Founders startup studio, and launched Immersive Futures (VR/AR consultancy). Also co-founded Coincall (crypto portfolio platform, sold 2019).

Danish executive. Master's in Applied Economics & Finance from Copenhagen Business School. Previously worked in sales and moved into startup world. Co-founded Synthesia with Victor Riparbelli in 2017.

36-year-old Chinese AI researcher and entrepreneur. Former researcher at SenseTime. Founded MiniMax in December 2021 with a vision for multimodal AGI. Led company's B2C-first approach and bet on multimodality from day one. Described as 'gentle,' 'Buddhist,' and 'low-key' but orchestrated one of the fiercest AI IPOs in history with 109% debut.
Former director of innovative business at SenseTime. Operational discipline leader with global orientation. Attended Johns Hopkins and Columbia Universities. Four years of experience in the US.
Co-founder of MiniMax. Part of founding team from SenseTime ecosystem. Involvement status could not be confirmed.
Co-founder of MiniMax. Part of founding team from SenseTime ecosystem. Involvement status could not be confirmed.

Polish entrepreneur, age 30. First-Class Honours in Mathematics from Imperial College London. Previously worked at Opera Software (Business Intelligence), BlackRock (Risk Analyst), and Palantir Technologies (Deployment Strategist, 2018-2022). Named to Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe (Technology) 2024 and TIME100 AI 2025. Led ElevenLabs to unicorn status in under 2 years.

Polish AI researcher, age 30. BA in Engineering from University of Oxford (with Distinction), MPhil in Advanced Computer Science from University of Cambridge. AI-based image detection thesis published at NeurIPS 2017. Previously worked at Opera Software, Tessian (cybersecurity), and Google (Software Engineer in Zürich, focusing on AI and large-scale systems, 2018-2022).

Canadian entrepreneur, age 32. Graduate of Industrial Engineering program at University of Toronto (2015). Previously co-founded ContentFly (a marketplace for freelance writers) with John Thomas in 2018, backed by Khosla Ventures. Wound down ContentFly after realizing LLMs would disrupt the business. Took sabbatical to study AI video generation research papers before founding Moonvalley.
Canadian entrepreneur. University of Toronto graduate. Co-founded ContentFly with Naeem Talukdar in 2018. Joined Moonvalley founding team in 2023.
Former Google DeepMind researcher in London. Worked on Google's text-to-video generator, Veo 1. Left Google to pursue independent venture due to slow product release cycles.
Former Google DeepMind researcher in London. Worked on Google's text-to-video generator, Veo 1. Co-founded Moonvalley to advance AI video generation technology.

Two-time Oscar-nominated filmmaker for documentary shorts. Co-founded Asteria (AI film studio) with Natasha Lyonne. Asteria was acquired by Moonvalley. Leads Moonvalley's movie-making unit Asteria Labs.

Uzbekistani-American entrepreneur. Former Head of Generative AI at Snap Inc., where he led development of AR effects, filters, MyAI chatbot, and Cameos Stories. Co-founded AI Factory (acquired by Snap for $166M in 2020). Ranked among top 3 in the world in competitive programming at age 20. Started programming at age 10. Parents are engineering professors (mother designed rockets for Soviet space program). Degrees from UC Berkeley, Innopolis University, and Moscow Institute of Physics and Technology (MIPT).
AI researcher specializing in generative video. Co-founded Higgsfield AI with Alex Mashrabov. Previously worked in AI research and development.
Co-founder of Higgsfield AI. Combination of deep product experience with technical strength.

Chinese-American entrepreneur, born circa 1999 in Hangzhou, China. BA in Mathematics and Master's in Computer Science from Harvard University. Silver medal at 2015 International Olympiad in Informatics. Dropped out of Stanford Computer Science PhD program in April 2023 to found Pika. Previously interned at Microsoft, Google, Meta AI, Google Brain, and Microsoft Research. Hailed as 'genius girl' in China. Mother graduated from MIT, father was former chairman of Sunyard Technology Group.

Chinese-American AI researcher. Dropped out of Stanford Computer Science PhD program alongside Demi Guo in April 2023. Previously conducted research in Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory focusing on generative models and diffusion models.

Former Apple engineer who worked on Vision Pro project and computer vision. Contributed to multimedia experiences for spatial computing. Brought product vision and technical expertise in AI and computer vision to Luma AI.

Former UC Berkeley AI researcher in Kanazawa's group. Authored influential papers on neural rendering techniques including pixelNeRF and PlenOctrees. Currently AI researcher at OpenAI. Former CTO of Luma AI until joining OpenAI. Expert in 3D scene representation and neural rendering.
Former Apple employee with experience in AR/VR systems engineering. Co-founded Luma AI in 2021 with vision for accessible 3D content creation.

Chinese-American entrepreneur. Master's in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University, BS in Electrical Engineering from Tongji University Shanghai. Former Software Engineer at Snapchat (2014-2020), one of first 100 employees. Built Snapchat advertising platform, recommendation algorithm system, machine learning platform Barista, and AI camera. Expert in ads ranking, machine learning, and computational photography.

Chinese-American product leader. Master's degree from Carnegie Mellon University, studied at Tongji University Shanghai with Joshua Xu. Former Product Designer at Smule (karaoke app) and ByteDance (TikTok parent company). Moved to West Coast in 2014. Drives product development and technological advancement at HeyGen.
Korean-American AI entrepreneur. Co-founded Twelve Labs in 2021 with vision for multimodal video understanding. Part of founding team building AI-based platform for video analysis.
AI entrepreneur and engineer. Co-founded Twelve Labs in 2021. Part of founding team building video understanding AI technology.
AI entrepreneur and engineer. Co-founded Twelve Labs in 2021. Part of founding team developing multimodal AI for video analysis.
AI entrepreneur and engineer. Co-founded Twelve Labs in 2021. Part of founding team building video understanding AI platform.
AI entrepreneur and engineer. Co-founded Twelve Labs in 2021. Part of founding team developing video AI technology.

Entrepreneur and marketer by trade. Y Combinator alum who previously co-founded Proof, a startup that used algorithms to customize websites. Led Jasper from launch to unicorn status. Stepped down as CEO in September 2023, remaining as Chairman.
Background in business-to-business marketing and process automation. Helped co-launch Proof before founding Jasper.
Background in business-to-business marketing and process automation. Helped co-launch Proof before founding Jasper.

PhD in Computer Science (Computer Vision) from Ben-Gurion University in collaboration with Harvard University. Former CTO of Trax Retail where he helped transform the company from an early-stage startup to unicorn status with ~1,000 employees. Holder of 60+ patents at the intersection of AI and commercial applications. Visionary in Computer Vision and Generative AI.
Co-founded Bria with Dr. Yair Adato in 2020. Works alongside the CEO to build the Responsible and Open Generative AI Platform.

French entrepreneur from La Bassée, France. Started as one of France's top eBay sellers at age 17. Master's from ESCP Business School with programs at Stanford and IIM Bangalore. Previously worked at Moodstocks (acquired by Google) and Mention. Forbes 30 Under 30 Europe 2017. Named one of Top 100 Most Influential French People by Vanity Fair 2021.
French entrepreneur and engineer. Met Clément Delangue in 2016 and shared passion for open technology. College friend of Thomas Wolf.
PhD in Physics. Machine learning researcher and author of research papers. Leads open-source and science teams at Hugging Face.

Former CTO of Adobe (2015-2022). Led Adobe's transformation to cloud and AI-powered experiences with Creative Cloud, Document Cloud, and Experience Cloud. Previously held leadership roles at Microsoft, Oracle, and i2 Technologies. Founded Typeface in 2022 after recognizing the need for enterprise-ready generative AI solutions for marketing.
